name: Pheasant a' L'orange - Country Living Holidays
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Rinse pheasants and pat dry. Remove excess fat from body and neck cavities. Reserve necks and giblets. Cut the peel and membrane off one orange; slice into 1/4-inch-thick pieces. Reserve the peel.
2. Place half of the orange slices on the breast meat under the skin of each bird. Tuck wing tips under the back to hold the neck skin down. Place one carrot quarter, one onion quarter, and half of the orange peel inside each body cavity. Tie the legs together with kitchen string.
3. Melt butter in a 2-quart saucepan; remove from heat. Place pheasants, breast sides up, on a rack in a large roasting pan. Brush birds all over with melted butter. Set aside any leftover butter.
4. Roast the birds for 30 minutes. In the same saucepan with leftover butter, brown reserved necks and giblets. Add remaining onion and carrot quarters; sauté 1 minute. Add water; bring to a boil. Partially cover pan and simmer giblet mixture over medium heat for 45 minutes or until liquid is reduced by almost a third.
5. Brush birds with pan drippings. Turn each bird on its side; cover breasts with pieces of aluminum foil. Continue roasting for 15 minutes. Turn birds to roast on the other side; roast for an additional 15 to 20 minutes, or until juices run clear when thighs are pierced with a fork and breast meat near the wing joint is white with a touch of pink but no longer wet and soft.
6. While pheasants are roasting, prepare rice following package directions, if desired. Remove birds from the roasting pan; discard string. Transfer to a serving platter; surround with hot cooked rice blend. Add giblet mixture to the roasting pan; heat and stir to loosen bits from the bottom. Strain into a measuring cup or bowl; discard particles. If less than 2 cups liquid, add water or chicken broth.
7. In a 2-quart saucepan, heat sugar and vinegar over medium-high heat until sugar dissolves. Continue boiling until golden brown or caramelized (3-5 minutes). Remove from heat; set aside. Combine wine and cornstarch in a cup; stir into the syrup in the saucepan. Whisk in strained liquid and salt; return to heat and boil, stirring constantly until thickened.
8. Brush some sauce over birds; pour remainder into a small pitcher. Peel and thinly slice remaining orange. Arrange slices on top of pheasant breasts. To carve, remove legs and thighs with a sharp knife. Cut drumsticks from thighs. Slice breasts off the bone. Serve with sauce.
